info.title: Monitor API Documentation
info.description: This file provides all documentation for the monitor API in the OpenAPI format.

servers.description: Server dynamically inferred from API documentation request.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The requested page of the paginated results.

parameters.name: pagesize
parameters.description: The number of items requested (if available) for the page of the paginated results.

parameters.name: sortby
parameters.description: Request a specific ordering of fields. Must be formatted as a comma separated list with `<fieldName>:<order>`, where order must be `asc` or `desc`. When the order is omitted, `asc` is used.

get.summary: Check if the user or session has the supplied access rights
Check if the user or session has an access right

serialNumber.description: EAN code for Fluvius, Inverter serialNumber for SolarEdge

emailAddress.description: E-mailadres to send the mandate request to (Fluvius)

apiAccount.description: The account to be used to connect to the API

identifier.description: Unique identifier for the device. This is only used for Fluvius connections and equals the EAN code of the meter

plantId.description: A technical reference to the API meter
The technical reference in the remote portal

apiAccount.description: The account to be used to connect to the API

type.description: The type of comment you want to add

date.description: The date on which is logically coupled to the Comment

resolution.description: Predefined list of solutions that can be linked to an alarm when solved

cause.description: Predefined list of causes that can be linked to an alarm

action.description: Predefined list of action types that can be linked to an alarm

priority.description: Predefined list of priorities to rank the importance of an alarm

status.description: Status of alarm (e.g. open or closed)

comment.description: List of comments that were added to alarm

created.description: Time when alarm was created in application

notifierTemplate.description: link to mail template that will be used

alarmPriority.description: The alarmpriority defines which thresholds should be used to create alarms. You can define different categories with different thresholds. (e.g. an installation with HIGH priority will receive a network alarm after 6 hours, while LOW priority will receive an alarm after 48 hours.)

communicator.description: The communicator serial number starting with **XMX** or **IQ**. If no Communicator is selected, the linked Communicator field of Installation is used as input.

description.description: Custom name that will be used to fill in Meter Type

initialPolling.description: You can choose which data to process for this particular device. All data (whole period) or only the data read after registration.

firstRegisteredOn.description: First time when measurement was linked to an meter

polling.description: Indicates if data is processed for this measurement (overrule from meter)

lastPolledValue.description: Last valid meter reading. (correspondig to the latest cumulative value registered by the physical device)

lastPolledRealtimeValue.description: Last realtime value that was processed

lastPolledRealtimeTimeStamp.description: Last realtime timestamp that was processed

aggregationType.description: Indicaties how data is aggregated (e.g. sum, avg, max, min)

firstRegisteredOn.description: First time when meter was linked to an installation

registeredOn.description: Time when meter was linked to an installation (if meter was unregistered/re-registered this will be updated)

lastCommunicationAttemptOn.description: Last attempt of network communication with physical device

lastSuccessfulCommunicationOn.description: Last successful network communication with physical device

polling.description: Indicates whether the device is processing data (e.g. yes or no)

lastPollingResult.description: Indicates whether the last data processing was completed without errors (e.g. successful)

lastSuccessfulPollingOn.description: Time when data was last processed for this device

created.description: Time when meter was created in the application

firmWareVersion.description: Version of the firmware of the device

firstRegisteredOn.description: Time the device was first registered

lastSuccessfulPollingOn.description: Time of last successful polling of the device

SimReferenceModel.description: Connection which can be used for network communication

testDurationPerMode.description: The duration per mode expressed in minutes

modbusActionType.description: A predefined lists of available modbus settings

modbusFunctionCode.description: The Modbus protocol offers a number of functions that are used to read or write data

slaveAddress.description: The master device addresses a specific slave device by using the address field. Valid RTU addresses are from 1-247

registerAddress.description: Modbus functions operate on register map registers to monitor, configure, and control module I/O. This value is often a 5-digit number (0xxxx,1xxxx,3xxxx,4xxxx)

registerValue.description: When executing a write function, the value to be written should be filled in.

registerLength.description: The amount of registers that will be read. In case of a larger datatype such as U32, multiple registers have to be read/written.

type.description: The type will indicate which action was requested (e.g. `connectiontest`)

status.description: Initial = The command is waiting for execution. Dispatching = We are trying to connect to the module. Dispatched = The action was executed.

result.description: Failure or Success. A failure is often caused by a connection issue and can be solved by a retry at a later moment.

serviceProvider.description: The service provider of the sim card (1NCE or Sim Services)

iccId.description: Unique serial number of the sim card

ipAddress.description: The IP Address to reach the sim card

networkStatus.description: The status of the sim on the network

enableInverterControl.description: Enables the virtual grid meter and allows control over the behavior of a hybrid inverter.

inverterControlFirstActivatedOn.description: The date when the inverter control was first activated.

batteryCapacity.description: The capacity, in kWh, of the installed batteries linked to the EMS.

batteryPower.description: The maximum (dis)charge power, in kW, of the installed batteries linked to the EMS.

enableEvChargerControl.description: Enables the EMS app for EV charger control and allows you to adjust the charging power based on grid usage and solar production.

evChargerControlFirstActivatedOn.description: The date when the EV charger control was first activated.

evNumberOfPhases.description: Number of phases configured in the EV charger controlled by the EMS.

maxGridCurrent.description: Maximum rated current of the grid connection in Amperes.

minChargeCurrent.description: Minimum charge current that should be used by the connected EV charger in Amperes.

userRequestedEvBatteryMode.description: Controls battery discharging when EV charging is active: 0 - Off (battery will not be used to support EV charging), 1 - On.

userRequestedMaxChargeCurrent.description: Maximum charging current (from grid or solar) that can be used by the connected EV charger in Amperes.

userRequestedMaxGridCurrent.description: Maximum charging current (imported from the grid) that can be used by the connected EV charger in Amperes.

enablePeakShaving.description: Enables the EMS app for capacity tariff and allows you to limit the maximum grid power by adjusting charging powers, activating the battery, or disabling external devices.

peakShavingFirstActivatedOn.description: The date when the peak shaving feature was first activated.

minBatteryChargeSoc.description: A value ranging from 0-100 that represents the minimum battery charge. This variable is set by the installer to ensure battery health.

userRequestedMaxGridPower.description: The maximum grid power. This variable can be controlled by the user.

userRequestedBatteryPeakReserveSoc.description: A value ranging from 0-100 that represents the battery reserve used to cover peak electricity usage. This variable can be controlled by the user.

enableRealtime.description: Enables the EMS app for real-time polling.

realtimeFirstActivatedOn.description: The date when the real-time polling feature was first activated.

enableDynamicPricing.description: Enables EMS apps for dynamic pricing

dynamicPricingFirstActivatedOn.description: The date when the dynamic pricing feature was first activated.

userRequestedLowerPriceThreshold.description: The threshold for the lower price. This variable can be controlled by the user.

userRequestedUpperPriceThreshold.description: The threshold for the upper price. This variable can be controlled by the user.

enableMbusReader.description: Enables the EMS app for reading mbus devices.

enableSmartGridReadyControl.description: Enables the EMS app for smart grid ready control.

smartGridReadyControlFirstActivatedOn.description: The date when the smart grid ready control feature was first activated.

userRequestedDeactivateHeatPumpOnHighPri.description: Deactivates the heat pump on high prices. This variable can be controlled by the user.

userRequestedActivateHeatPumpOnLowPrices.description: Activates the heat pump on low prices. This variable can be controlled by the user.

increasedThreshold.description: Consumption of the heat pump in Watt when it is in increased operation.

forcedThreshold.description: Consumption of the heat pump in Watt when it is in forced operation.

performedOn.description: De datum waarop de switch breaker succesvol werd uitgevoerd.

status.description: De status waarin het command zich bevindt. Dit veld bepaalt door welke taak de switch breaker wordt gebruikt.

breakerOn.description: Het veld dat bepaald of de breaker aan of uit gezet wordt.

testStartOn.description: Time of first connection with the `communicator` and start of test

startExportValue.description: Cumulative export value in Wh as reported on the display of `communicator`

startImportValue.description: Cumulative import value in Wh as reported on the display of `communicator`. This value should be close to 0 and lower than the export value. If not, this might indicate incorrect wiring.

nextExportValue.description: Cumulative export value that was read from the `communicator` after several minutes. The difference between the start and next value is used to decide if a valid production was measured.

averageVoltage.description: Average voltage of the last 10 minutes at the moment of the productiontest. If this value is close to 253V, this might lead to over voltage which might to a shutdown of the inverter.

instantaneousActivePowerImport.description: imported power measured by the `communicator` at the moment of productiontest. If this value is greater as 0, the `communicator` might be wired incorrectly.

instantaneousActivePowerExport.description: exported power measured by the communicator at the moment of productiontest. If this value is greater as 0, the installation is working as expected.

orientation.description: Orientation of the installed solar panels, 0 is North, 90 is East, 180 is South, and 270 is West.

tilt.description: Tilt of the installed solar panels, 0 is horizontal, and 90 is vertical.

wattpeak.description: Maximum power of installad solar panels. Expressed in Wp.

expectedProduction.description: Expected energy production in one year for this specific installation. Can also be used to register the guaranteed production.

cspiEnabled.description: Enable this to activate the CSPI algorithm. Orientation, tilt, wattpeak and location/zip should be filled in before your installation can be analyzed.

panelClassification.description: Type of silicon solar cell that is used.

inverterClassification.description: Type of inverter that is used. e.g. micro-inverter, string-inverter

clientApiUser.description: OAuth2 connect with ClientApiUser credentials. Login into SIMPL to create new credentials.
